title: a join causes  SIGSEGV after creation of Unique Key
poc: CREATE TABLE mmtest10 ( a1 varchar(4) NOT NULL, a2 bigint NOT NULL, a3 bigint NOT NULL, a4 bigint NOT NULL );
CREATE TABLE mmtest20 ( a1 varchar(9) NOT NULL, a2 bigint NOT NULL, a3 bigint NOT NULL, a4 bigint NOT NULL );
ALTER TABLE mmtest20 ADD CONSTRAINT mmtest20_uk1 UNIQUE (a1,a2,a3,a4);
SELECT p.a1, p.a2, p.a3, v.a3 FROM mmtest10 v JOIN mmtest20 p ON ( p.a1 = v.a1 AND p.a2 = v.a2 AND p.a3 = v.a3 AND p.a4 = v.a4 );
key_bug_features: join operation after unique key creation
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE mmtest30 ( b1 varchar(5) NOT NULL, b2 int NOT NULL, b3 float NOT NULL, b4 date NOT NULL );
CREATE TABLE mmtest40 ( b1 varchar(10) NOT NULL, b2 int NOT NULL, b3 float NOT NULL, b4 date NOT NULL );
ALTER TABLE mmtest40 ADD CONSTRAINT mmtest40_uk1 UNIQUE (b1,b2,b3,b4);
INSERT INTO mmtest30 (b1, b2, b3, b4) VALUES ('test', 1, 1.1, '2023-01-01');
SELECT p.b1, p.b2, p.b3, v.b3 FROM mmtest30 v JOIN mmtest40 p ON ( p.b1 = v.b1 AND p.b2 = v.b2 AND p.b3 = v.b3 AND p.b4 = v.b4 );
